user.title: Aeolus
user.description: This creates the Aeolus manager. After you press Submit, open the new Aeolus entry and use **+ Add** to create **Spaces** (zones you want to manage by CO₂) and **Actuators** (fans, vents, ERV or windows that lower CO₂). Nothing is controlled until you add and configure those.

abort.already_configured: Aeolus is already set up. Add Spaces and Actuators from the Aeolus entry's ⋮ menu.

user.title: Add a space
user.description: A **Space** is a zone managed by CO₂ (e.g. Primary Bedroom). Choose its CO₂ sensor(s) and the levels Aeolus should hold.

data_description.name: A friendly name for this zone — it becomes the device name.
data_description.co2_sensors: One or more CO₂ sensors in this zone. Several are smoothed together into one reading.
data_description.aggregation: How to combine multiple sensors: Mean = average; Median = ignores a single outlier; Min/Max = best / worst-case.
data_description.target_ppm: Ventilation stops once the zone reaches this. ~800 ppm is good indoor air; fresh outdoor air is ~420 ppm.
data_description.high_ppm: Ventilation starts when the zone rises above this. Must be higher than the target — the gap between them is the deadband that prevents short-cycling.
data_description.volume_ft3: Optional, and currently NOT used. Reserved for the planned occupancy/generation (equilibrium-CO₂) estimate. The air-change-rate readout is gap-normalized and does not need volume.
data_description.outdoor_aq_entity: Optional. A PM2.5 sensor measuring this zone's outdoor air. When it's high, Aeolus won't pull outdoor air in.
data_description.outdoor_aq_threshold: Optional. Block outdoor-air ventilation when the outdoor PM sensor exceeds this value.

reconfigure.title: Edit space
reconfigure.description: Update this zone's sensors and CO₂ levels.

data_description.name: A friendly name for this zone — it becomes the device name.
data_description.co2_sensors: One or more CO₂ sensors in this zone. Several are smoothed together into one reading.
data_description.aggregation: How to combine multiple sensors: Mean = average; Median = ignores a single outlier; Min/Max = best / worst-case.
data_description.target_ppm: Ventilation stops once the zone reaches this. ~800 ppm is good indoor air; fresh outdoor air is ~420 ppm.
data_description.high_ppm: Ventilation starts when the zone rises above this. Must be higher than the target.
data_description.volume_ft3: Optional, currently not used (reserved for the planned generation/equilibrium-CO₂ estimate). The ACH readout is gap-normalized — volume-free.
data_description.outdoor_aq_entity: Optional. A PM2.5 sensor measuring this zone's outdoor air.
data_description.outdoor_aq_threshold: Optional. Block outdoor-air ventilation when the outdoor PM sensor exceeds this value.

user.title: Add an actuator
user.description: An **Actuator** lowers CO₂ by moving air — a fan, vent, ERV, or window opener. Note: recirculating air purifiers (HEPA/carbon) do **not** remove CO₂, so don't add those here.

data_description.name: A friendly name for this device.
data_description.actuator_entity: The fan, switch, input_boolean, or cover Aeolus turns on/off.
data_description.mechanism: Balanced = ERV/HRV, neutral pressure. Supply = pushes outdoor air in. Exhaust = pulls air out (depressurizes the home). Transfer = moves air between rooms. Window = an opener/cover.
data_description.served_spaces: Which Space(s) this device ventilates — Aeolus runs it when one of them needs fresh air. (Create Spaces first; they appear in this list.)
data_description.filter_efficiency: How well this device's intake filters fine particles: 0 = none/foam, ~0.5 = MERV-13, 0.9+ = HEPA. A filtered intake is allowed to run when outdoor air is dirtier.
data_description.rearm_interval: For a fan/switch whose load auto-offs on its own while the switch keeps reporting 'on', re-send the ON command this often (minutes) while still needed. 0 = off. Set just under the hardware auto-off — e.g. 14 for a fan that auto-offs at ~15–30 min.
data_description.on_speed_pct: Fans only — the speed Aeolus sets when it turns this fan on (0 = leave at the fan's default). The fan snaps to its nearest step, e.g. a 5-speed range hood → 20/40/60/80/100%.
data_description.override_grace_min: How long a hand/automation change must persist before Aeolus yields control. 0 = react immediately (default). Set ~2 for cloud devices (e.g. LG ThinQ) whose state briefly flaps to off/unavailable, so a 1-min cloud blip isn't mistaken for you taking over.

reconfigure.title: Edit actuator
reconfigure.description: Update this device, how it moves air, and the spaces it serves.

data_description.name: A friendly name for this device.
data_description.actuator_entity: The fan, switch, input_boolean, or cover Aeolus turns on/off.
data_description.mechanism: Balanced = ERV/HRV, neutral pressure. Supply = pushes outdoor air in. Exhaust = pulls air out. Transfer = moves air between rooms. Window = an opener/cover.
data_description.filter_efficiency: Intake fine-particle filtration: 0 = none/foam, ~0.5 = MERV-13, 0.9+ = HEPA.
data_description.rearm_interval: Re-send the ON command this often (minutes) for a load that auto-offs internally. 0 = off. ~14 for the Primary-Bath toilet fan.
data_description.on_speed_pct: Fans only — % speed Aeolus sets on turn-on (0 = fan default). Snaps to the fan's step.
data_description.override_grace_min: Minutes a manual change must persist before Aeolus yields. 0 = immediate. ~2 for cloud actuators (LG ThinQ) to ignore transient flaps.

recalibrate.name: Recalibrate
recalibrate.description: Clear observed/learned actuator effectiveness for an Aeolus entry.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry
config_entry_id.description: The Aeolus config entry to recalibrate.
